jokes.text: Will, you're just Nicky's cousin, I'm his brother. I'm a big branch of his family tree. Then why are your limbs so short?
jokes.setup: Carlton brags about being a 'big branch' of the family tree
jokes.punchline: Will's comeback about Carlton's short limbs/height
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure using Carlton's height as the punchline. Well-integrated character comedy that could only work with Carlton.

jokes.text: Don't forget your flag, though. What's a day at the Nixon Library without Old Glory?
jokes.setup: Will pretends to be helpful about Carlton's Nixon Library trip
jokes.punchline: The reveal that Will is mocking Carlton's patriotic earnestness
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor playing on Carlton's earnest patriotism and Will's street-smart mockery.

jokes.text: Everybody going to the beach say, 'Kowabunga!' Kowabunga! Hey!
jokes.setup: Will tricks Carlton into enthusiastically saying 'Kowabunga' thinking they're going to the beach
jokes.punchline: Carlton's realization he's been duped (implied by his 'Hey!')
jokes.explanation: Classic Will-tricking-Carlton moment with physical comedy of Carlton's enthusiastic response followed by realization.

jokes.text: Man, now who does a thing like that? I know, can you believe her?
jokes.setup: Will asks who would send themselves flowers after being upset
jokes.punchline: Hilary thinks he's referring to Leeza Gibbons, not her narcissistic behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke that perfectly captures Hilary's self-centeredness. She completely misses that Will is criticizing her behavior.

jokes.text: I'll get her. And her little car too.
jokes.setup: Hilary's revenge plot against Leeza Gibbons
jokes.punchline: Reference to Wizard of Oz witch's threat but about a car
jokes.explanation: Nice pop culture reference adapted to the situation. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Will's come a long way from Philly, but let's face it... he's still a common street hood from the wrong side of the tracks. So will you help me?
jokes.setup: Hilary insults Will as a street hood
jokes.punchline: Then immediately asks for his help with her scheme
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hilary's oblivious entitlement - insulting Will then asking for his criminal expertise.

jokes.text: Oh, gee, you know, when you put it that way, no.
jokes.setup: Hilary's insulting request for help
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic response to being called a street hood
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to Hilary's tactless approach. Economical and well-delivered.

jokes.text: Dude came out, started that joint, it was like 'kaboom!' Boom!
jokes.setup: Will describes pranking a gym teacher with a potato in the tailpipe
jokes.punchline: The explosive result with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy bit. Not particularly original but fits Will's character and advances the plot.

jokes.text: What a wonderful lesson for Master Nicky. Perhaps now you could teach him to run with scissors.
jokes.setup: Will's story about vandalism in front of young Nicky
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry suggestion about other dangerous activities to teach
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey line with perfect British dry wit. The escalation to 'running with scissors' is well-crafted and quotable.

jokes.text: Geoffrey, if I were a potato, where would I be?
jokes.setup: Hilary needs to find a potato for her revenge scheme
jokes.punchline: Her absurd philosophical approach to potato location
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist line that perfectly captures Hilary's airheaded logic. Highly quotable and could only come from her character.

jokes.text: Yeah, me and Will are going trolling for slimmies.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to sound cool about going to the beach
jokes.punchline: His awkward use of slang that doesn't quite work
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton trying to be cool and failing. The cringe factor is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Look, Ashley, it's Daddy's mad face.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil's disapproval of the beach conversation
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact commentary on Phil's expression
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observational humor. Will's casual commentary on family dynamics.

jokes.text: Come on, Nicky, let's go upstairs before Daddy's head explodes.
jokes.setup: Phil's increasing anger
jokes.punchline: Ashley's exaggerated description of potential explosion
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor and escalation of the tension. Ashley's practicality adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Oh, kind of like Michael Bolton.
jokes.setup: Phil says Nicky is like a sponge, imitating what he hears
jokes.punchline: Will's comparison to Michael Bolton's derivative music
jokes.explanation: Sharp, unexpected comparison that works on multiple levels. Excellent economy and structure for a quick pop culture reference.

jokes.text: Will locked me in the closet. That's so tired.
jokes.setup: Carlton arrives late and disheveled
jokes.punchline: The reveal of Will's prank followed by Will's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling prank comedy. Will's 'that's so tired' response adds character flavor.

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry, Mom, it only happened once. You meant Nicky, didn't you?
jokes.setup: Vivian worries about Nicky being exposed to 'too much sex'
jokes.punchline: Ashley's innocent but inappropriate response about her own experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke with perfect awkward timing. Ashley's innocent delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: Oh. Dougie? That big, dumb, orange whale that be singing them stupid songs?
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions Dougie the children's entertainer
jokes.punchline: Will's harsh but accurate description
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Will's street-smart cynicism versus the family's wholesome entertainment choices.

jokes.text: Those are life-affirming little ditties... that bring joy to the young and young at heart.
jokes.setup: Will's harsh description of Dougie's songs
jokes.punchline: Carlton's over-the-top earnest defense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - overly earnest and using formal language to defend children's entertainment.

jokes.text: Now, can we think about this for a second, please? Now, Dougie loves everything. People, am I the only one that finds something terribly wrong with that?
jokes.setup: The family wants to take Nicky to see Dougie
jokes.punchline: Will's logical but dark critique of unconditional love
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversive take on children's entertainment. Will's street-smart cynicism applied to wholesome content creates great comedy.

jokes.text: I love bugs and I love death, I love oozing flesh wounds
jokes.setup: Will's critique of Dougie loving everything
jokes.punchline: Taking the 'love everything' concept to its dark extreme
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy that perfectly illustrates Will's point. The escalation from bugs to death to flesh wounds is expertly crafted and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Mommy! Mommy! Will! Will! What the hell is wrong with you?
jokes.setup: Will's dark parody of Dougie's song
jokes.punchline: Nicky's traumatized reaction and Phil's outrage
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Will's dark comedy. The escalation from Nicky's trauma to Phil's anger works well.

jokes.text: Well, he is a whale. Maybe we should go look for him at the beach.
jokes.setup: Nicky asks where Dougie is at the mall
jokes.punchline: Will's literal interpretation of Dougie being a whale
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan logic joke. Will's literal thinking applied to the costume character.

jokes.text: Hey, someone check the damn level.
jokes.setup: Dougie performing his wholesome children's song
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift to adult performer voice complaining about sound
jokes.explanation: Excellent breaking of the fourth wall/character. The contrast between Dougie's persona and the performer is sharp and well-executed.

jokes.text: That's it. You SOBs better pull your thumbs out... and get your freaking acts together.
jokes.setup: Second microphone feedback during Dougie's performance
jokes.punchline: Even more profane outburst from the children's entertainer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous joke. The contrast becomes even more extreme with stronger language.

jokes.text: I think something must have crawled up his blowhole.
jokes.setup: Nicky asks what's wrong with Dougie
jokes.punchline: Will's whale anatomy joke about Dougie's bad mood
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining whale anatomy with the common expression. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Last time a Dougie concert was canceled, kids went crazy and trashed a babyGap.
jokes.setup: The crowd is getting restless about Dougie
jokes.punchline: Carlton's absurd historical reference to baby clothes store destruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy. The specificity of 'babyGap' and the image of children rioting over a whale character is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go back and have a mammal-to-mammal with Dougie.
jokes.setup: Will decides to talk to Dougie about his behavior
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'man-to-man' talk but with mammal since Dougie is a whale
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that shows Will's quick wit. The pun works perfectly in context and is highly quotable.

jokes.text: Aren't you a little old for this crap?
jokes.setup: Will politely approaches Dougie about returning to perform
jokes.punchline: Dougie's harsh, adult response to Will's reasonable request
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing the performer's cynicism. Creates sympathy for Will while building tension.

jokes.text: Then the little brat got his money's worth. Now beat it.
jokes.setup: Will explains Nicky got in free
jokes.punchline: Dougie's cruel logic about free admission
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works by subverting expectations. The performer's callousness toward children is shocking but funny.

jokes.text: Um, look here, you big orange Moby Dick.
jokes.setup: Dougie's rude behavior pushes Will to respond
jokes.punchline: Will's literary insult combining the costume with classic literature
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that shows Will's intelligence. Combining street attitude with literary reference works perfectly.

jokes.text: I know this isn't as glamorous as your job down at the mini-mart...
jokes.setup: Dougie responds to Will's challenge
jokes.punchline: Assuming Will works at a convenience store
jokes.explanation: Standard class-based insult. Sets up the confrontation but not particularly original.

jokes.text: You pushed me, called me 'punk.' That's funny.
jokes.setup: Physical confrontation is building
jokes.punchline: Will's dangerous calm before the storm
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's street background. The calm delivery makes it more threatening.

jokes.text: Oh, it's on now.
jokes.setup: Dougie continues to provoke Will
jokes.punchline: Will's street fighting declaration
jokes.explanation: Classic confrontation line. Not original but fits Will's character perfectly and sets up the physical comedy.

jokes.text: Okay, time for another song. What are you doing to Dougie?
jokes.setup: Will is fighting with Dougie behind the scenes
jokes.punchline: Carlton's oblivious attempt to cover followed by child's concern
jokes.explanation: Good situational comedy with Carlton trying to maintain the illusion while violence occurs off-screen.

jokes.text: Oh. We was just playing, y'all. Hey, sorry. I need a drink. You hurt Dougie. I hate you, Will.
jokes.setup: Will's fight with Dougie is exposed
jokes.punchline: Multiple levels of aftermath - Will's weak excuse, Dougie's need for alcohol, Nicky's devastation
jokes.explanation: Excellent multi-layered joke with escalating consequences. Each character's reaction adds to the comedy and plot development.

jokes.text: Get him! No.
jokes.setup: Children are angry at Will for hurting Dougie
jokes.punchline: Carlton's failed attempt to control the mob
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective escalation. The image of children chasing Will while Carlton tries to stop them works.

jokes.text: Nicky and I are gonna be spending more time together, especially since he's... M-A-D at W-I-L-L.
jokes.setup: Carlton is taking advantage of Nicky being mad at Will
jokes.punchline: His childish spelling to avoid saying it directly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's opportunistic but childish nature. The spelling is appropriately immature.

jokes.text: Mommy, I H-A-T-E them.
jokes.setup: Carlton's spelling game with Nicky
jokes.punchline: Nicky copying the spelling pattern for his own complaint
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Nicky learning the spelling game but using it to reject Carlton's makeover.

jokes.text: Heh. Really? It's hard to imagine you being quiet for that long, Aunt Viv.
jokes.setup: Vivian mentions she stopped speaking to Will's mother for weeks
jokes.punchline: Will's gentle roast of Vivian's talkative nature
jokes.explanation: Nice character-based humor that shows the family dynamics. Will's affectionate teasing of Vivian.

jokes.text: Didn't you chop the head off her teddy bear and flush it down the toilet? That's how I got over it.
jokes.setup: Vivian mentions getting over being mad at Will's mother
jokes.punchline: The reveal of her extremely violent revenge method
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy reveal. The escalation from 'got over it' to extreme violence is perfectly crafted and subverts expectations about Vivian.

jokes.text: Wielding? I was not wielding, I was rubbing. I rubbed that damn potato all over Leeza's car... and it didn't make a scratch.
jokes.setup: Newspaper describes Hilary 'wielding a potato'
jokes.punchline: Hilary's indignant correction and admission of ineffective vandalism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to the potato joke with escalating absurdity. Hilary's focus on the wrong issue while admitting failure is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: At least they didn't catch me putting my keys in her exhaust pipe.
jokes.setup: Hilary's relief about the potato incident coverage
jokes.punchline: Revealing she did something worse that wasn't discovered
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke and callback to Will's earlier story. Shows Hilary actually listened and applied the criminal advice.

jokes.text: Hilary, you were suppo... Never mind.
jokes.setup: Hilary admits to putting keys in the exhaust pipe
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's realization she did it wrong and giving up on correcting her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey reaction showing his exasperation. The 'never mind' suggests he was about to explain the potato-in-tailpipe method but gave up.

jokes.text: Oh, right, the guy inside Dougie.
jokes.setup: Will tries to explain he fought with the person in the costume
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sarcastic dismissal of Will's explanation
jokes.explanation: Carlton's disbelief in Will's excuse delivered with perfect skepticism

jokes.text: That damn whale got him brainwashed.
jokes.setup: Will tries to explain about fighting with the person inside Dougie
jokes.punchline: Will's frustrated assessment of Dougie's influence on Nicky
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's frustration with the situation he created. The 'brainwashed' comment shows his street perspective.

jokes.text: Yeah, Shaft. And how did you feel when you found out that Shaft wasn't real? What are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Ashley asks about Will's childhood heroes
jokes.punchline: Will's confusion when Ashley suggests Shaft isn't real
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Will's street background and genuine confusion about fiction vs. reality regarding his hero.

jokes.text: No, I'm saying, he was based on an actual guy. No, he wasn't. He was too, Ashley. Shaft is fictional, Will. I'm saying, he went to Africa and everything.
jokes.setup: Debate about whether Shaft is real
jokes.punchline: Will's increasingly desperate attempts to prove Shaft's reality with made-up details
jokes.explanation: Great escalating argument with Will doubling down on his misconception. The 'Africa' detail shows his desperate improvisation.

jokes.text: No, I'm saying, he went to Africa and everything. / Shaft is fictional, Will. / I'm saying, he went to Africa and everything.
jokes.setup: Ashley tries to explain Shaft isn't real
jokes.punchline: Will doubles down with specific 'facts' about Shaft's travels
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's sincere belief in Shaft's reality

jokes.text: In rehab.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks where Dougie is now
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact delivery about the children's entertainer's substance abuse treatment
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy moment. The single word delivers maximum impact, subverting expectations about children's entertainers. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: Yes, but I so rarely have a woman scream my name... I was rather enjoying it.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks why Geoffrey didn't respond to her calling
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sexual innuendo about enjoying being screamed at
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey line with his signature dry wit and subtle adult humor. Perfect character integration with his loneliness and British sensibility.

jokes.text: Geoffrey, if I knew how to use the VCR, would I need this camera? No, I would not.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey suggests using the VCR instead of a camera
jokes.punchline: Hilary's flawed logic delivered with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary logic failure delivered with total confidence. Her technological incompetence is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Ooh, that witch. So you see it too. All I see is a beautiful woman... perched atop two of the most finely sculpted legs I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Hilary calls Leeza Gibbons a witch, Geoffrey agrees he 'sees it too'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey was looking at Leeza's physical attributes, not her personality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with Geoffrey's characteristic appreciation for attractive women. The formal language adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Oh, like those are real.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey admires Leeza Gibbons' legs
jokes.punchline: Hilary's catty suggestion about plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Standard catty comment that fits Hilary's character but isn't particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa, the pogmeister's in the house. Who wants some?
jokes.setup: Carlton enters ready to play pogs
jokes.punchline: His overconfident announcement about a children's game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment showing his earnest enthusiasm for childish things. The 'pogmeister' title is appropriately cringe.

jokes.text: Gets that from his mother.
jokes.setup: Will says he's not in the mood to play
jokes.punchline: Carlton's casual insult toward Will's mother
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's casual meanness when he's feeling confident. The economy of the insult works well.

jokes.text: Any other normal kid, you could just buy their love back.
jokes.setup: Will is frustrated about Nicky being mad at him
jokes.punchline: Will's cynical observation about children and materialism
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation with dark humor about parenting and children's values. Shows Will's street-smart perspective on family dynamics.

jokes.text: Well, now, Will, as much as the idea of your reproducing scares the hell out of me...
jokes.setup: Will says he'll never have kids
jokes.punchline: Phil's backhanded support admitting Will having kids terrifies him
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phil line combining support with honest assessment. The structure builds to the insult then pivots to encouragement.

jokes.text: How come you don't ever do that? Don't question me.
jokes.setup: Phil advises about admitting when you're wrong and apologizing
jokes.punchline: Will points out Phil's hypocrisy, Phil's authoritarian response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction showing Will's boldness and Phil's defensive authority. The quick shut-down is perfectly Phil.

jokes.text: Wow. I didn't know you could read the paper. I can't.
jokes.setup: Will finds Nicky with a newspaper
jokes.punchline: Nicky's honest admission he can't actually read
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with perfect economy. Nicky's matter-of-fact delivery of 'I can't' is perfectly timed and childlike.

jokes.text: Here, read the personals, Nicky. Yo, hottie. Yo.
jokes.setup: Will stops himself from telling Nicky about fictional characters
jokes.punchline: Will's desperate deflection to inappropriate newspaper section
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's poor judgment in trying to distract Nicky. The personal ad reading is appropriately inappropriate.

jokes.text: Freeze, munchkins! They're not munchkins, son, they're elves.
jokes.setup: Will catches what he thinks are burglars
jokes.punchline: Santa's correction about his helpers' proper classification
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the Santa reveal with appropriate fantasy creature confusion. Santa's pedantic correction is in character.

jokes.text: Who are you, Keebler? Oh, boy, you're not even close.
jokes.setup: Will challenges the intruder's identity
jokes.punchline: Will's cookie elf reference and Santa's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference with Will's street-smart approach to the situation. Santa's response sets up the reveal.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. Santa Claus. Right on, yeah. Everybody knows Santa Claus is a Clippers fan. I am not. I just can't move these things.
jokes.setup: Will sarcastically acknowledges 'Santa'
jokes.punchline: Santa's explanation for his Lakers gear
jokes.explanation: Good LA-specific sports humor with Santa's practical explanation. Will's skepticism is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Maybe this year I'll put a brain in your stocking.
jokes.setup: Santa notes Will doesn't understand what he's talking about
jokes.punchline: Santa's Christmas-themed insult about Will's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Excellent Christmas-themed insult that's both clever and character-appropriate. Highly quotable and well-structured.

jokes.text: Why do these make-believe guys keep pushing me?
jokes.setup: Second fictional character has physically confronted Will
jokes.punchline: Will's recognition of a pattern in his encounters
jokes.explanation: Great callback and pattern recognition. Will's confused observation about fictional characters being aggressive is genuinely funny and quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, here, here. You be a good boy. And tell Carlton to stop faxing me. I cannot get him into Princeton.
jokes.setup: Santa is leaving after his speech to Will
jokes.punchline: Santa's casual mention of Carlton's college admission requests
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist joke that perfectly captures Carlton's character through Santa's complaint. The modern technology (faxing) combined with impossible request is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: You know Santa? Know him? Who do you think gave him the idea for that mistletoe thing?
jokes.setup: Nicky asks if Will knows Santa
jokes.punchline: Will's outrageous claim about inventing Christmas traditions
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's tendency to exaggerate and make himself important. The mistletoe claim is appropriately outrageous.

jokes.text: See, we was chilling back in Philly one night. It was me, Santa, the tooth fairy and Shaft, right? Who's Shaft?
jokes.setup: Will continues his story about knowing fictional characters
jokes.punchline: Including Shaft in the group and Nicky's innocent question
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Shaft debate with escalating absurdity. Nicky's innocent question sets up the final character moment.

jokes.text: See, Shaft was a complicated man.
jokes.setup: Nicky asks who Shaft is
jokes.punchline: Will's appropriately vague but accurate description from the theme song
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback that references the Shaft theme song while being appropriately child-friendly. Good character resolution showing Will's growth.

jokes.text: See, Shaft was a complicated man.
jokes.setup: Nicky asks who Shaft is
jokes.punchline: Will quotes the movie's famous line as if explaining to a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback ending with famous Shaft theme lyric delivered to innocent child
